speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Lord Bishop, set the crown upon his head.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: God save King Henry, of that name the Sixth!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Now, Governor of Paris, take your oath.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That you elect no other king but him;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Esteem none friends but such as are his friends,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And none your foes but such as shall pretend

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Malicious practices against his state:

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: This shall you do, so help you righteous God.

speaker: FASTOLF
text: My gracious sovereign, as I rode from Callice

speaker: FASTOLF
text: To haste unto your coronation,

speaker: FASTOLF
text: A letter was delivered to my hands,

speaker: FASTOLF
text: Writ to your Grace from th’ Duke of Burgundy.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Shame to the Duke of Burgundy and thee!

speaker: TALBOT
text: I vowed, base knight, when I did meet thee next,

speaker: TALBOT
text: To tear the Garter from thy craven’s leg,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Which I have done, because unworthily

speaker: TALBOT
text: Thou wast installèd in that high degree.—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Pardon me, princely Henry and the rest.

speaker: TALBOT
text: This dastard, at the battle of Patay,

speaker: TALBOT
text: When but in all I was six thousand strong

speaker: TALBOT
text: And that the French were almost ten to one,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Before we met or that a stroke was given,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Like to a trusty squire did run away;

speaker: TALBOT
text: In which assault we lost twelve hundred men.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Myself and divers gentlemen besides

speaker: TALBOT
text: Were there surprised and taken prisoners.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Then judge, great lords, if I have done amiss,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Or whether that such cowards ought to wear

speaker: TALBOT
text: This ornament of knighthood—yea or no?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To say the truth, this fact was infamous

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And ill beseeming any common man,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Much more a knight, a captain, and a leader.

speaker: TALBOT
text: When first this Order was ordained, my lords,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Knights of the Garter were of noble birth,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Valiant and virtuous, full of haughty courage,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Such as were grown to credit by the wars;

speaker: TALBOT
text: Not fearing death nor shrinking for distress,

speaker: TALBOT
text: But always resolute in most extremes.

speaker: TALBOT
text: He then that is not furnished in this sort

speaker: TALBOT
text: Doth but usurp the sacred name of knight,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Profaning this most honorable Order,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And should, if I were worthy to be judge,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Be quite degraded, like a hedge-born swain

speaker: TALBOT
text: That doth presume to boast of gentle blood.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Stain to thy countrymen, thou hear’st thy doom.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Be packing therefore, thou that wast a knight.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Henceforth we banish thee on pain of death.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And now, my lord protector, view the letter

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Sent from our uncle, Duke of Burgundy.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What means his Grace that he hath changed his style?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text:
No more but, plain and bluntly, 
                “
                To
                 
                the
                 
                King
                ”
              !

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Hath he forgot he is his sovereign?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Or doth this churlish superscription

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Pretend some alteration in good will?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What’s here?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text:
I
                 
                have
                 
                upon
                 
                especial
                 
                cause
                ,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Moved with compassion of my country’s wrack,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Together with the pitiful complaints

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Of such as your oppression feeds upon,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Forsaken your pernicious faction

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And joined with Charles, the rightful king of France.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: O monstrous treachery! Can this be so?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That in alliance, amity, and oaths

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: There should be found such false dissembling guile?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What? Doth my Uncle Burgundy revolt?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: He doth, my lord, and is become your foe.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Is that the worst this letter doth contain?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: It is the worst, and all, my lord, he writes.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, then, Lord Talbot there shall talk with him

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And give him chastisement for this abuse.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How say you, my lord, are you not content?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Content, my liege? Yes. But that I am prevented,

speaker: TALBOT
text: I should have begged I might have been employed.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Then gather strength and march unto him straight;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Let him perceive how ill we brook his treason

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And what offense it is to flout his friends.

speaker: TALBOT
text: I go, my lord, in heart desiring still

speaker: TALBOT
text: You may behold confusion of your foes.

speaker: VERNON
text: Grant me the combat, gracious sovereign.

speaker: BASSET
text: And me, my lord, grant me the combat too.

speaker: YORK
text: This is my servant; hear him, noble prince.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And this is mine, sweet Henry; favor him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Be patient, lords, and give them leave to speak.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Say, gentlemen, what makes you thus exclaim,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And wherefore crave you combat, or with whom?

speaker: VERNON
text: With him, my lord, for he hath done me wrong.

speaker: BASSET
text: And I with him, for he hath done me wrong.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What is that wrong whereof you both complain?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: First let me know, and then I’ll answer you.

speaker: BASSET
text: Crossing the sea from England into France,

speaker: BASSET
text: This fellow here with envious carping tongue

speaker: BASSET
text: Upbraided me about the rose I wear,

speaker: BASSET
text: Saying the sanguine color of the leaves

speaker: BASSET
text: Did represent my master’s blushing cheeks

speaker: BASSET
text: When stubbornly he did repugn the truth

speaker: BASSET
text: About a certain question in the law

speaker: BASSET
text: Argued betwixt the Duke of York and him,

speaker: BASSET
text: With other vile and ignominious terms.

speaker: BASSET
text: In confutation of which rude reproach,

speaker: BASSET
text: And in defense of my lord’s worthiness,

speaker: BASSET
text: I crave the benefit of law of arms.

speaker: VERNON
text: And that is my petition, noble lord;

speaker: VERNON
text: For though he seem with forgèd quaint conceit

speaker: VERNON
text: To set a gloss upon his bold intent,

speaker: VERNON
text: Yet know, my lord, I was provoked by him,

speaker: VERNON
text: And he first took exceptions at this badge,

speaker: VERNON
text: Pronouncing that the paleness of this flower

speaker: VERNON
text: Bewrayed the faintness of my master’s heart.

speaker: YORK
text: Will not this malice, Somerset, be left?

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Your private grudge, my Lord of York, will out,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Though ne’er so cunningly you smother it.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Good Lord, what madness rules in brainsick men

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When for so slight and frivolous a cause

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Such factious emulations shall arise!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Good cousins both, of York and Somerset,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Quiet yourselves, I pray, and be at peace.

speaker: YORK
text: Let this dissension first be tried by fight,

speaker: YORK
text: And then your Highness shall command a peace.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: The quarrel toucheth none but us alone;

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Betwixt ourselves let us decide it then.

speaker: YORK
text: There is my pledge; accept it, Somerset.

speaker: VERNON
text: Nay, let it rest where it began at first.

speaker: BASSET
text: Confirm it so, mine honorable lord.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Confirm it so? Confounded be your strife,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And perish you with your audacious prate!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Presumptuous vassals, are you not ashamed

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: With this immodest clamorous outrage

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To trouble and disturb the King and us?—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And you, my lords, methinks you do not well

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To bear with their perverse objections,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Much less to take occasion from their mouths

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To raise a mutiny betwixt yourselves.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Let me persuade you take a better course.

speaker: EXETER
text: It grieves his Highness. Good my lords, be friends.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come hither, you that would be combatants:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Henceforth I charge you, as you love our favor,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Quite to forget this quarrel and the cause.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And you, my lords, remember where we are:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: In France, amongst a fickle wavering nation.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If they perceive dissension in our looks,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And that within ourselves we disagree,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How will their grudging stomachs be provoked

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To willful disobedience and rebel!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Besides, what infamy will there arise

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When foreign princes shall be certified

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That for a toy, a thing of no regard,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: King Henry’s peers and chief nobility

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Destroyed themselves and lost the realm of France!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, think upon the conquest of my father,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My tender years, and let us not forgo

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That for a trifle that was bought with blood.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Let me be umpire in this doubtful strife.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I see no reason if I wear this rose

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That anyone should therefore be suspicious

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I more incline to Somerset than York.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Both are my kinsmen, and I love them both.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As well they may upbraid me with my crown

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Because, forsooth, the King of Scots is crowned.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But your discretions better can persuade

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Than I am able to instruct or teach;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And therefore, as we hither came in peace,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So let us still continue peace and love.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Cousin of York, we institute your Grace

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To be our regent in these parts of France;—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And good my Lord of Somerset, unite

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Your troops of horsemen with his bands of foot;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And like true subjects, sons of your progenitors,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Go cheerfully together and digest

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Your angry choler on your enemies.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ourself, my lord protector, and the rest,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: After some respite, will return to Callice;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: From thence to England, where I hope ere long

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To be presented, by your victories,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With Charles, Alanson, and that traitorous rout.

speaker: WARWICK
text: My Lord of York, I promise you the King

speaker: WARWICK
text: Prettily, methought, did play the orator.

speaker: YORK
text: And so he did, but yet I like it not

speaker: YORK
text: In that he wears the badge of Somerset.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Tush, that was but his fancy; blame him not.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I dare presume, sweet prince, he thought no harm.

speaker: YORK
text: And if iwis he did—but let it rest.

speaker: YORK
text: Other affairs must now be managèd.

speaker: EXETER
text: Well didst thou, Richard, to suppress thy voice,

speaker: EXETER
text: For had the passions of thy heart burst out,

speaker: EXETER
text: I fear we should have seen deciphered there

speaker: EXETER
text: More rancorous spite, more furious raging broils,

speaker: EXETER
text: Than yet can be imagined or supposed.

speaker: EXETER
text: But howsoe’er, no simple man that sees

speaker: EXETER
text: This jarring discord of nobility,

speaker: EXETER
text: This shouldering of each other in the court,

speaker: EXETER
text: This factious bandying of their favorites,

speaker: EXETER
text: But sees it doth presage some ill event.

speaker: EXETER
text: ’Tis much when scepters are in children’s hands,

speaker: EXETER
text: But more when envy breeds unkind division:

speaker: EXETER
text: There comes the ruin; there begins confusion.
